message:
fcp-store: fix WAL seq gap on transient append failure

record_mutation used fetch_add to reserve the next seq before calling
append_wal_record. If the WAL append failed (ENOSPC, EACCES, EIO,
read-only remount), next_seq had already advanced, so the subsequent
successful mutation wrote a seq that skipped one slot. load_wal_records
detects the gap and refuses to load, leaving the store permanently
unrecoverable on the next restart.

Load-then-store so next_seq only advances after the append is durable.
Both DurableObjectStore and DurableSymbolStore are affected; write_guard
already serializes record_mutation, so the non-atomic load/store is
race-free.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
